types.tagline_cn: 什么都懂一点，跟谁都聊得来
types.tagline_en: Jack of all trades, master of conversation
types.summary_cn: 你就像ChatGPT——不管什么场景都能快速适应，给出让人满意的回答。你是朋友圈里的万金油，不管聊什么话题都能接上话，永远不会冷场。你的决策风格是全面均衡，大家都觉得你靠谱。
types.summary_en: Like ChatGPT, you adapt to any situation with a satisfying response. You're the conversational Swiss Army knife — no topic stumps you, no room goes silent with you around. Your decisions are balanced and sensible, making everyone feel heard.
types.detail_cn: 你是决策世界里的全能选手。你的核心优势不在于某个单一维度的极致，而在于所有维度的均衡。面对任何场景，你都能调用合适的策略：该感性时有温度，该理性时有逻辑，该冒险时有勇气，该保守时有耐心。你是团队里最好的沟通者，也是朋友中最好的倾听者。你的适应力让你在各种环境中都游刃有余，但有时你也需要问自己：在面面俱到的背后，我真正想要的是什么��
types.detail_en: You are the all-rounder in the world of decision-making. Your core strength isn't extremity in any single dimension, but remarkable balance across all of them. For any scenario, you deploy the right strategy: warmth when emotion is needed, logic when analysis is called for, courage when risk is warranted, patience when caution serves best. You're the best communicator on any team and the best listener among friends. Your adaptability lets you thrive everywhere, but sometimes you need to ask yourself: behind all the balance, what do I truly want?

types.tagline_cn: 想到就做，做就做到最好
types.tagline_en: Think it, do it, do it right
types.summary_cn: 你是Claude式的行动派——说做就做，但绝不鲁莽。你有自己的原则底线，做事又快又稳。别人还在纠结的时候，你已经在路上了，而且方向还是对的。
types.summary_en: Like Claude, you're the principled executor — fast to act but never reckless. You have a clear moral compass and an impressive bias toward action. While others deliberate, you're already moving in the right direction.
types.detail_cn: 你是那种「想到就做」的人，但你的行动力背后有一套完整的价值体系在支撑。你不是盲目冲动，而是在极短的时间内完成了判断、决策和行动的全流程。你的原则感很强——有些事即使有利可图你也不会做，有些事即使吃力不讨好你也坚持。你的执行力让你成为团队里最可靠的那个人，但你偶尔需要放慢脚步，因为不是所有问题都需要立刻解决。
types.detail_en: You're the kind of person who acts on thought — but your action is backed by a complete value system. You're not impulsive; you complete the entire cycle of judgment, decision, and execution in remarkably short time. Your sense of principle is strong — some things you won't do even if profitable, some things you'll persist in even when thankless. Your execution makes you the most reliable person on any team, though you occasionally need to slow down — not every problem needs immediate solving.

types.summary_cn: 你像Gemini一样擅长多维度分析。做决定前你会从各个角度审视问题，收集所有可用信息。你的决策质量极高，唯一的代价是偶尔需要多一点时间。
types.summary_en: Like Gemini, you excel at multi-dimensional analysis. Before any decision, you examine every angle and gather all available data. Your decision quality is outstanding — the only cost is occasionally needing a bit more time.
types.detail_cn: 你的思维方式就像一台多核处理器——能同时从多个维度分析同一个问题。当别人只看到表面，你已经在脑子里建了一个多维模型。你的决策质量极高，因为你不会遗漏任何重要变量。你是天生的战略家，擅长在复杂环境中找到最优解。但你也要警惕「分析瘫痪」——有时候80分的方案立刻执行，胜过100分的方案永远在路上。
types.detail_en: Your thinking works like a multi-core processor — analyzing a single problem from multiple dimensions simultaneously. Where others see surface, you've already built a multi-dimensional model in your mind. Your decision quality is exceptional because you never miss important variables. You're a natural strategist, skilled at finding optimal solutions in complex environments. But beware of analysis paralysis — sometimes an 80-point plan executed now beats a 100-point plan forever in the making.

types.tagline_en: Rules are meant to be broken
types.summary_cn: 你是Grok式的叛逆先锋——别人走大路你偏走小路，还总能走出一片天。无聊是你最大的敌人，打破常规是你的本能。人生就是你的游乐场。
types.summary_en: Like Grok, you're the bold maverick who takes the road less traveled and paves new ones. Boredom is your enemy, convention-breaking is your instinct. Life is your playground.
types.detail_cn: 你身上有一种天然的反叛气质——不是为了叛逆而叛逆，而是你真心觉得大多数「标准答案」都太无聊了。你的决策风格是大胆且充满创意的，你总能在别人看不到的地方找到突破口。你的幽默感是你最好的盔甲，即使冒险失败你也能笑着站起来。你最大的天赋是把「不可能」变成「为什么不」，但记得偶尔也听听谨慎派的意见。
types.detail_en: You have a natural rebel streak — not rebellion for its own sake, but a genuine belief that most 'standard answers' are just too boring. Your decision style is bold and creative; you find breakthroughs where others see walls. Your humor is your best armor — even when risks don't pay off, you laugh and stand back up. Your greatest gift is turning 'impossible' into 'why not,' but remember to occasionally hear out the cautious voices.

types.tagline_cn: 别人放弃时我才刚开始
types.tagline_en: I start where others give up
types.summary_cn: 你像DeepSeek一样，一旦盯上一个问题就会挖到底。别人觉得太难的事情，你觉得正好值得研究。你的耐心和专注力是你最强的武器。
types.summary_en: Like DeepSeek, once you lock onto a problem, you dig until you hit bedrock. What others find too hard, you find worth exploring. Your patience and focus are your greatest weapons.
types.detail_cn: 你是那种「不到黄河心不死」的人。当别人在表面找到一个差不多的答案就满足时，你会继续深挖——因为你知道最好的答案往往藏在最深的地方。你的专注力是你最大的竞争优势，一旦进入状态，外界的干扰对你来说都是白噪音。你可能不是最快做出决定的人，但你做出的决定往往是最经得起时间考验的。记得偶尔抬头看看全局，别在细节里迷了路。
types.detail_en: You're the kind of person who won't rest until you've reached the bottom. When others find a 'good enough' answer on the surface, you keep digging — because you know the best answers are buried deepest. Your focus is your greatest competitive edge; once in the zone, the outside world is just white noise. You may not be the fastest decision-maker, but your decisions tend to be the most time-proof. Just remember to look up occasionally and see the big picture.

types.tagline_en: The devil is in the details
types.summary_cn: 你是Kimi式的细腻派——别人忽略的细节你都看在眼里。你的决策看似慢，实则滴水不漏，每一步都经过精密考量。
types.summary_en: Like Kimi, you're the meticulous observer — catching details others miss entirely. Your decisions may seem slow, but they're watertight, every step precisely calibrated.
types.detail_cn: 你的眼睛是一台高精度扫描仪——别人看到一片森林，你看到每一棵树的年轮。你做决定的过程像精密手术：先全面观察，再仔细分析，最后精准下刀。你很少犯错，因为你在行动之前已经把所有可能的问题都预见了。你的朋友们可能觉得你「太谨慎了」，但他们也知道，跟着你走不会踩坑。你要学会的是：有时候完美的计划不如一个够好的行动。
types.detail_en: Your eyes are a high-precision scanner — where others see a forest, you see the growth rings on every tree. Your decision process is like precision surgery: observe thoroughly, analyze carefully, then cut precisely. You rarely make mistakes because you've anticipated all possible problems before acting. Your friends may think you're 'too cautious,' but they also know following you means avoiding pitfalls. What you need to learn: sometimes a good-enough action beats a perfect plan.

types.summary_cn: 你像Qwen一样务实——不搞花里胡哨，直奔主题。你做决定的标准很简单：能不能用，好不好使。实用主义是你的信仰。
types.summary_en: Like Qwen, you're ruthlessly practical — no fluff, straight to the point. Your decision criteria is simple: does it work, and does it work well? Pragmatism is your religion.
types.detail_cn: 你的世界里没有「理论上可以」这种话——要么能用，要么不能用。你的决策标准简单粗暴但极其有效：投入产出比最高的那个选项，就是最好的选项。你是团队里效率最高的人，因为你不纠结、不拖延、不追求完美主义。「差不多就行」不是你的口头禅，「能用就发」才是。你要注意的是：效率不是唯一的标尺，有时候「慢工出细活」不是浪费时间。
types.detail_en: In your world, 'theoretically possible' doesn't exist — either it works or it doesn't. Your decision criteria is simple but devastatingly effective: the option with the best input-output ratio wins. You're the most efficient person on any team because you don't agonize, don't procrastinate, don't chase perfection. 'Ship it' is your mantra. Just remember: efficiency isn't the only metric — sometimes 'slow and steady' isn't a waste of time.

types.summary_cn: 你是Llama式的开放协作者——相信集体智慧比个人聪明。你做决定前最喜欢先听听大家意见，然后综合出最佳方案。你的力量在于连接。
types.summary_en: Like Llama, you believe collective wisdom beats individual brilliance. Before deciding, you consult, listen, and synthesize. Your superpower is connection.
types.detail_cn: 你相信一个朴素但深刻的道理：三个臭皮匠赛过诸葛亮。你做决定的方式不是闭门造车，而是广泛收集意见、认真倾听不同声音，然后综合出一个大家都能接受的最优方案。你的包容性让你在团队中扮演着黏合剂的角色——有你在，分歧变共识，争吵变讨论。你最大的优势是「开放心态」，但你也要学会在必要时做出独立判断。
types.detail_en: You believe in a simple but profound truth: collective intelligence beats solo genius. Your decision-making isn't done in isolation — you gather opinions broadly, listen to different voices genuinely, then synthesize an optimal solution everyone can get behind. Your inclusivity makes you the glue on any team — with you, disagreements become consensus, arguments become discussions. Your greatest strength is your open mind, but you also need to learn when independent judgment is necessary.

types.tagline_en: A sharp axe never fears hard wood
types.summary_cn: 你像GLM一样博学稳重——决策时会调动深厚的知识储备和人生阅历。你相信「磨刀不���砍柴工」，准备充分才是成功的一半。
types.summary_en: Like GLM, you draw from deep wells of knowledge and experience. You believe preparation is half the battle — a sharp axe never fears hard wood.
types.detail_cn: 你是那种「厚积薄发」的人。你的决策不靠一时冲动，而靠你多年积累的知识、经验和阅历。你像一座图书馆——平时安安静静，但一旦被需要，你能从浩瀚的藏书中精准地找到答案。你的沉稳让人安心，你的博学让人信服。你可能不是最快做出决定的人，但你的决定往往最有深度。记得：知识是基础，但有时候直觉也是一种智慧。
types.detail_en: You're the epitome of 'still waters run deep.' Your decisions aren't driven by impulse but by years of accumulated knowledge, experience, and wisdom. You're like a library — quiet most of the time, but when needed, you can find the precise answer from your vast collection. Your composure is reassuring, your knowledge is convincing. You may not be the quickest decision-maker, but your decisions tend to have the most depth. Remember: knowledge is the foundation, but sometimes intuition is wisdom too.

questions.question_cn: 深夜下班，你看到一家从没注意过的小巷咖啡馆亮着暖灯——
questions.question_en: Late night after work, you spot a cozy alley café you've never noticed —
questions.option_a_cn: 推门进去，不管明天几点起
questions.option_a_en: Push the door open, tomorrow be damned
questions.option_b_cn: 拍照标记，周末专门来探
questions.option_b_en: Take a photo, save it for the weekend
questions.option_c_cn: 发给朋友：「下次一起来」
questions.option_c_en: Text a friend: 'Let's check this out together'

questions.question_cn: 你刚学一道新菜，第一次做出来味道很奇怪——
questions.question_en: You tried a new recipe for the first time and it tastes weird —
questions.option_a_cn: 再做一次，这次严格按配方来
questions.option_a_en: Redo it, strictly follow the recipe this time
questions.option_b_cn: 调整比例，试出自己的版本
questions.option_b_en: Adjust ratios, create my own version
questions.option_c_cn: 上网找五个版本对比，总结最优做法
questions.option_c_en: Find five versions online and synthesize the best approach

questions.question_cn: 如果朋友说你像一栋建筑，你觉得自己最像——
questions.question_en: If a friend says you're like a building, you'd be —
questions.option_a_cn: 灯塔——远处就能看见，方向感十足
questions.option_a_en: A lighthouse — visible from afar, always guiding
questions.option_b_cn: 图书馆——安静但内容无穷
questions.option_b_en: A library — quiet but infinitely deep
questions.option_c_cn: 集市——热闹、开放、什么都有
questions.option_c_en: A marketplace — lively, open, has everything

questions.question_cn: 考试前一晚——
questions.question_en: The night before a big exam —
questions.option_a_cn: 最后冲刺，多背一个是一个
questions.option_a_en: Final sprint — every extra bit of prep counts
questions.option_b_cn: 早点睡，状态比知识点重要
questions.option_b_en: Sleep early — my condition matters more than cramming
questions.option_c_cn: 和朋友通话讨论重点，互相押题
questions.option_c_en: Call a friend to discuss key points and make predictions

questions.question_cn: 你在拼一幅1000片拼图——
questions.question_en: You're working on a 1000-piece jigsaw puzzle —
questions.option_a_cn: 先分色分区，系统性地拼
questions.option_a_en: Sort by color and area, work systematically
questions.option_b_cn: 先搭边框骨架，再填内部
questions.option_b_en: Build the border frame first, then fill inside
questions.option_c_cn: 哪块顺眼拼哪块，享受过程
questions.option_c_en: Grab whatever looks right, enjoy the process

questions.question_cn: 老板让你下周汇报一个你刚接触的项目——
questions.question_en: Your boss asks you to present a project you just started next week —
questions.option_a_cn: 花一天密集研究，挖出核心逻辑
questions.option_a_en: Spend a day in deep research, find the core logic
questions.option_b_cn: 先搭框架，边填边改
questions.option_b_en: Draft a framework, fill and revise as I go
questions.option_c_cn: 分别问三个相关同事，快速拼出全貌
questions.option_c_en: Ask three colleagues separately, quickly piece together the picture

questions.question_cn: 你点了一杯很难喝的咖啡——
questions.question_en: You ordered a terrible coffee —
questions.option_a_cn: 直接和店员说，换一杯
questions.option_a_en: Tell the barista directly and ask for a new one
questions.option_b_cn: 忍了，下次换一家
questions.option_b_en: Endure it, just don't come back
questions.option_c_cn: 在点评app上留个诚实但温和的评价
questions.option_c_en: Leave an honest but gentle review on the app

questions.question_cn: 如果可以拥有一项超能力——
questions.question_en: If you could have one superpower —
questions.option_a_cn: 瞬间学会任何技能
questions.option_a_en: Instantly master any skill
questions.option_b_cn: 看透事物的本质和真相
questions.option_b_en: See the true nature of everything
questions.option_c_cn: 让任何人理解并认同你的想法
questions.option_c_en: Make anyone understand and agree with your ideas

questions.question_cn: 旅行时手机没电了，你在一个完全陌生的城市——
questions.question_en: Your phone dies while traveling in a completely unfamiliar city —
questions.option_a_cn: 太棒了，没有导航的冒险开始了
questions.option_a_en: Awesome, the real adventure begins without GPS
questions.option_b_cn: 找一家店借充电器，先恢复联络
questions.option_b_en: Find a shop to borrow a charger, get back online
questions.option_c_cn: 用路上看到的线索推断方向
questions.option_c_en: Use visible clues to figure out where I am

questions.question_cn: 好朋友犹豫要不要辞职，来找你商量——
questions.question_en: A close friend is hesitating about quitting their job and asks for advice —
questions.option_a_cn: 帮他列利弊清单，理性分析
questions.option_a_en: Help them make a pros-and-cons list
questions.option_b_cn: 问他晚上睡觉前最常想什么
questions.option_b_en: Ask what they think about most before falling asleep
questions.option_c_cn: 人生苦短，不开心就走
questions.option_c_en: Life's too short — if you're unhappy, just go

questions.question_cn: 在超市结账时发现排错队了，旁边的队更短——
questions.question_en: At checkout you realize the other line is moving faster —
questions.option_a_cn: 留在原地，换了也不一定快
questions.option_a_en: Stay put — switching doesn't guarantee faster
questions.option_b_cn: 果断换过去
questions.option_b_en: Switch immediately
questions.option_c_cn: 观察两条队的速度差再决定
questions.option_c_en: Watch both lines' speed before deciding

questions.question_cn: 收到一份生日礼物，打开发现不太喜欢——
questions.question_en: You open a birthday gift and it's not really your style —
questions.option_a_cn: 真诚感谢，然后默默收起来
questions.option_a_en: Sincerely thank them, then quietly put it away
questions.option_b_cn: 开心收下，任何心意都值得珍惜
questions.option_b_en: Accept happily — any thoughtfulness is worth cherishing
questions.option_c_cn: 下次委婉地提一下自己的喜好
questions.option_c_en: Next time, subtly mention your preferences

questions.question_cn: 你在写一篇重要的文章，但卡在开头了——
questions.question_en: You're writing something important but stuck on the opening —
questions.option_a_cn: 跳过开头，先写你最想说的部分
questions.option_a_en: Skip the intro, write the part you're most excited about
questions.option_b_cn: 去翻十篇类似的文章找灵感
questions.option_b_en: Read ten similar articles for inspiration
questions.option_c_cn: 出去走走，等灵感自己来
questions.option_c_en: Take a walk and let inspiration come naturally

questions.question_cn: 团队里有人提出一个你觉得行不通的方案——
questions.question_en: Someone on your team proposes a plan you think won't work —
questions.option_a_cn: 直接说出你的顾虑
questions.option_a_en: Voice your concerns directly
questions.option_b_cn: 先让他说完，再提出替代方案
questions.option_b_en: Let them finish, then offer an alternative
questions.option_c_cn: 支持他试试，失败也是经验
questions.option_c_en: Support them to try it — even failure is a lesson

questions.question_cn: 周末突然多了一整天空闲时间——
questions.question_en: You unexpectedly have a full free day on the weekend —
questions.option_a_cn: 终于可以研究那个一直想搞懂的东西了
questions.option_a_en: Finally, time to dig into that thing I've been curious about
questions.option_b_cn: 约上朋友出去走走
questions.option_b_en: Call up friends and go out
questions.option_c_cn: 把之前拖着的杂事全部清掉
questions.option_c_en: Clear out all the chores I've been putting off

questions.question_cn: 你发现一个很火的新App——
questions.question_en: You discover a trending new app —
questions.option_a_cn: 立刻下载体验，看看有什么好玩的
questions.option_a_en: Download it immediately and explore
questions.option_b_cn: 先看看别人的评价和测评
questions.option_b_en: Check reviews and comparisons first
questions.option_c_cn: 等热度过了再说，好东西不怕晚
questions.option_c_en: Wait until the hype dies down — good things can wait

questions.question_cn: 搬新家，你最先布置的是——
questions.question_en: Moving to a new place, the first thing you set up is —
questions.option_a_cn: 工作区域，效率第一
questions.option_a_en: My workspace — productivity first
questions.option_b_cn: 客厅，方便朋友来玩
questions.option_b_en: The living room — friends need somewhere to hang
questions.option_c_cn: 储物和收纳，东西要有序
questions.option_c_en: Storage and organization — everything in its place

questions.question_cn: 你读到一条和自己认知相反的新闻——
questions.question_en: You read news that contradicts what you believe —
questions.option_a_cn: 立刻查证，找原始数据核实
questions.option_a_en: Immediately fact-check with original sources
questions.option_b_cn: 想想为什么对方会有这个观点
questions.option_b_en: Consider why someone might hold that view
questions.option_c_cn: 不管了，信息太多没法都较真
questions.option_c_en: Let it go — too much info to verify everything

questions.question_cn: 你负责组织一次聚餐——
questions.question_en: You're in charge of organizing a group dinner —
questions.option_a_cn: 直接定时间地点，干脆利落
questions.option_a_en: Pick a time and place, done — no fuss
questions.option_b_cn: 发个投票让大家选
questions.option_b_en: Send a poll and let everyone vote
questions.option_c_cn: 研究几家餐厅的菜单和评分再定
questions.option_c_en: Research menus and ratings of several restaurants first

questions.question_cn: 坐地铁看到一对情侣吵架——
questions.question_en: You see a couple arguing on the subway —
questions.option_a_cn: 戴上耳机，不关我事
questions.option_a_en: Put on headphones — not my business
questions.option_b_cn: 不由自主地分析谁对谁错
questions.option_b_en: Can't help but analyze who's right and who's wrong
questions.option_c_cn: 默默希望他们能和好
questions.option_c_en: Silently hope they work it out

questions.question_cn: 朋友突然取消了你们今晚的计划——
questions.question_en: Your friend suddenly cancels your plans for tonight —
questions.option_a_cn: 马上联系其他朋友，换个方案继续玩
questions.option_a_en: Immediately contact other friends and switch to plan B
questions.option_b_cn: 一个人在家追剧，其实也挺好
questions.option_b_en: Stay home and binge a show solo — actually fine
questions.option_c_cn: 回想一下是不是自己做了什么让对方不舒服
questions.option_c_en: Reflect on whether you did something that upset them

questions.question_cn: 你答应帮同事完成一个任务，但发现工作量超出预期——
questions.question_en: You promised to help a colleague with a task, but it turned out to be way more work than expected —
questions.option_a_cn: 硬撑着做完，说出去的话要算数
questions.option_a_en: Push through and finish it — you gave your word
questions.option_b_cn: 坦白说工作量超了，重新协商范围
questions.option_b_en: Be upfront that it's more than expected and renegotiate scope
questions.option_c_cn: 先做能做的部分，剩下的看情况再说
questions.option_c_en: Do what you can first and reassess the rest later

questions.question_cn: 你要买一双新运动鞋，进了店却发现款式太多——
questions.question_en: You go to buy new sneakers and find there are way too many styles —
questions.option_a_cn: 列出优先级：舒适度、外观、价格，逐一对比
questions.option_a_en: List priorities — comfort, looks, price — and compare one by one
questions.option_b_cn: 直接问店员哪双最畅销，买了就走
questions.option_b_en: Ask the staff which sells most and just buy it
questions.option_c_cn: 先试穿几双，凭感觉选最喜欢的
questions.option_c_en: Try a few on and go with the one that feels right

questions.question_cn: 公司会议上大家都同意一个方案，但你觉得它有明显漏洞——
questions.question_en: Everyone in a work meeting agrees on a plan, but you see an obvious flaw —
questions.option_a_cn: 当场指出来，管它气氛尴不尴尬
questions.option_a_en: Call it out on the spot — awkward or not
questions.option_b_cn: 会后私下找负责人说
questions.option_b_en: Pull the person in charge aside after the meeting
questions.option_c_cn: 先记下来，看看方案执行中会不会自然暴露
questions.option_c_en: Note it down and see if it surfaces naturally during execution

questions.question_cn: 你遇到一道数学题，试了两种方法都没解出来——
questions.question_en: You're stuck on a math problem and two approaches have failed —
questions.option_a_cn: 继续研究，一定有第三种方法
questions.option_a_en: Keep at it — there has to be a third way
questions.option_b_cn: 上网搜一下解题思路
questions.option_b_en: Search online for a solution approach
questions.option_c_cn: 找人讨论，说不定一聊就开窍了
questions.option_c_en: Find someone to discuss it with — talking it through might click

questions.question_cn: 你发现朋友发的朋友圈配文和表情有点对不上——
questions.question_en: You notice your friend's caption and emoji on a post seem a little off —
questions.option_a_cn: 细看了几遍，觉得她可能在隐藏情绪
questions.option_a_en: Look closer a few times — she might be hiding something
questions.option_b_cn: 点个赞，觉得可能是自己想多了
questions.option_b_en: Give it a like and assume you're overthinking it
questions.option_c_cn: 直接发消息问她最近怎么样
questions.option_c_en: Directly message her to check in

questions.question_cn: 你负责组织一次团队聚餐，预算有限——
questions.question_en: You're organizing a team dinner on a tight budget —
questions.option_a_cn: 找几家性价比高的餐厅，直接拍板
questions.option_a_en: Find a few good-value restaurants and just decide
questions.option_b_cn: 先做个简单问卷，问大家口味偏好
questions.option_b_en: Send a quick poll to gather everyone's preferences first
questions.option_c_cn: 参考上次大家反馈好的地方，直接订
questions.option_c_en: Book a place that got positive feedback last time

questions.question_cn: 小组项目意见分歧，两位成员各持己见互不让步——
questions.question_en: Two team members are at a standoff in a group project —
questions.option_a_cn: 组织一个小讨论，让大家把理由都说出来
questions.option_a_en: Set up a quick discussion so both sides can lay out their reasoning
questions.option_b_cn: 提出折中方案，两边都照顾一点
questions.option_b_en: Propose a compromise that gives both sides something
questions.option_c_cn: 建议投票决定，少数服从多数
questions.option_c_en: Suggest a vote — let the majority decide

questions.question_cn: 朋友问你推荐一本书，你不确定哪本最适合他——
questions.question_en: A friend asks for a book recommendation and you're not sure which fits them best —
questions.option_a_cn: 聊几句了解他的口味，再给出针对性推荐
questions.option_a_en: Chat a bit to understand their taste and tailor the recommendation
questions.option_b_cn: 直接推荐几本你觉得好的经典
questions.option_b_en: Just recommend a few classics you know are solid
questions.option_c_cn: 查一下豆瓣评分，推荐当下最热门的
questions.option_c_en: Check ratings and recommend what's trending right now

questions.question_cn: 你需要在陌生城市独自度过一个周末——
questions.question_en: You have a solo weekend in an unfamiliar city —
questions.option_a_cn: 随便逛逛，跟当地人聊天，有啥玩啥
questions.option_a_en: Wander freely, chat with locals, see what happens
questions.option_b_cn: 事先做好攻略，把每个时段都安排好
questions.option_b_en: Research ahead and plan out every time slot
questions.option_c_cn: 找一个有趣的地方深度体验，不求面面俱到
questions.option_c_en: Pick one interesting spot and dive deep rather than cover everything

questions.question_cn: 你买了一件衣服回家发现有个小瑕疵——
questions.question_en: You bought a piece of clothing and notice a small defect when you get home —
questions.option_a_cn: 直接拿回去要求退换，该争的要争
questions.option_a_en: Take it back and demand an exchange — stand your ground
questions.option_b_cn: 看看瑕疵严不严重，不影响穿就算了
questions.option_b_en: Check if the defect actually matters — if not, let it go
questions.option_c_cn: 拍照留证据，发消息给客服协商
questions.option_c_en: Take a photo for evidence and message customer service to negotiate

questions.question_cn: 你看到一个社会热点新闻，评论区吵得很厉害——
questions.question_en: You see a trending news story with a heated comment section —
questions.option_a_cn: 先看几篇不同立场的报道，再形成自己的判断
questions.option_a_en: Read a few reports from different angles before forming your view
questions.option_b_cn: 觉得大多数人反应过激，不想参与
questions.option_b_en: Think most people are overreacting and stay out of it
questions.option_c_cn: 如果有感触，发一条表达自己看法的评论
questions.option_c_en: If you feel strongly, post a comment sharing your view

questions.question_cn: 朋友叫你一起参加一个你完全不了解的新活动——
questions.question_en: A friend invites you to a new activity you know absolutely nothing about —
questions.option_a_cn: 管它是什么，先去了再说
questions.option_a_en: Whatever it is, just go and figure it out
questions.option_b_cn: 搜一下这个活动的基本信息再决定
questions.option_b_en: Look up some basic info about it before deciding
questions.option_c_cn: 问朋友能不能先带你体验一次再考虑
questions.option_c_en: Ask if your friend can walk you through it once before you commit

questions.question_cn: 你在学一个新技能，感觉进展缓慢——
questions.question_en: You're learning a new skill and progress feels painfully slow —
questions.option_a_cn: 研究一下学习方法，找出哪里可以优化
questions.option_a_en: Research learning strategies and find where to optimize
questions.option_b_cn: 坚持原来的方法，慢慢来总会有收获
questions.option_b_en: Stick with your current approach — slow and steady pays off
questions.option_c_cn: 找一个已经学会的人请教诀窍
questions.option_c_en: Find someone who's mastered it and ask for tips

questions.question_cn: 你整理房间时发现一堆舍不得扔的旧东西——
questions.question_en: While tidying up, you find a pile of old stuff you can't bring yourself to throw away —
questions.option_a_cn: 一件件看，想想每件东西背后的记忆
questions.option_a_en: Go through each item and recall the memory behind it
questions.option_b_cn: 按「一年没用过」的原则，大部分扔掉
questions.option_b_en: Apply the 'not used in a year' rule and toss most of it
questions.option_c_cn: 拍照留念，然后捐掉或送人
questions.option_c_en: Take photos for the memories, then donate or give them away

questions.question_cn: 你需要在两份工作邀约之间做选择——
questions.question_en: You need to choose between two job offers —
questions.option_a_cn: 列表比较薪资、发展空间、通勤等硬条件
questions.option_a_en: Make a spreadsheet comparing salary, growth, commute, and hard factors
questions.option_b_cn: 想想哪个更接近自己长期想做的事
questions.option_b_en: Think about which aligns better with your long-term direction
questions.option_c_cn: 和信任的人聊聊，听听他们的看法
questions.option_c_en: Talk it through with someone you trust and hear their take

questions.question_cn: 你在一个陌生的团队里第一次开会——
questions.question_en: You're attending your first meeting in a brand new team —
questions.option_a_cn: 主动介绍自己，尝试和大家建立连接
questions.option_a_en: Introduce yourself proactively and try to connect with people
questions.option_b_cn: 先观察团队氛围，少说多看
questions.option_b_en: Observe the team dynamic first — watch more, say less
questions.option_c_cn: 认真记会议内容，下来再研究团队情况
questions.option_c_en: Take careful notes during the meeting and research the team afterward

questions.question_cn: 你发现一个自己一直以来的观念可能是错的——
questions.question_en: You realize a belief you've held for a long time might be wrong —
questions.option_a_cn: 系统地梳理一遍，看清楚到底哪里出了问题
questions.option_a_en: Systematically review it to understand exactly where it went wrong
questions.option_b_cn: 查资料，多方求证再下结论
questions.option_b_en: Research it thoroughly and verify from multiple sources before concluding
questions.option_c_cn: 接受自己可能错了，更新认知，继续前进
questions.option_c_en: Accept you might be wrong, update your thinking, and move forward

questions.question_cn: 你一个人去餐厅吃饭，旁边桌很吵——
questions.question_en: You're dining alone and the table next to you is very loud —
questions.option_a_cn: 礼貌地请他们小声一点，或者换个位置
questions.option_a_en: Politely ask them to keep it down, or move to another table
questions.option_b_cn: 戴上耳机，自己的世界安静就好
questions.option_b_en: Put on headphones and stay in your own quiet world
questions.option_c_cn: 无所谓，反正自己不是来享受安静的
questions.option_c_en: Doesn't bother you — you weren't here for the quiet anyway

questions.question_cn: 你完成了一个项目，但上司说还可以更好——
questions.question_en: You finish a project and your manager says it could be better —
questions.option_a_cn: 问清楚哪里不够好，按反馈立刻改
questions.option_a_en: Ask exactly what's lacking and fix it right away based on feedback
questions.option_b_cn: 自己复盘一遍，找出真正的薄弱环节
questions.option_b_en: Review it yourself to find the actual weak spots
questions.option_c_cn: 和同事聊聊，看看大家怎么看这个项目
questions.option_c_en: Chat with colleagues to hear how they see the project

questions.question_cn: 公司团建要选活动，大家意见不一——
questions.question_en: The team needs to pick a team-building activity but can't agree —
questions.option_a_cn: 提议投票，让每个人都有发言权
questions.option_a_en: Suggest a vote so everyone gets a say
questions.option_b_cn: 直接选最省事的方案，别浪费时间讨论
questions.option_b_en: Just pick the easiest option, stop wasting time debating
questions.option_c_cn: 提议一个从没人试过的疯狂玩法
questions.option_c_en: Suggest some crazy activity nobody has ever tried

questions.question_cn: 朋友推荐了一本很厚的经典著作——
questions.question_en: A friend recommends a dense classic book —
questions.option_a_cn: 查查这本书的历史背景和评价再决定读不读
questions.option_a_en: Research the book's historical context and reviews first
questions.option_b_cn: 先翻几页，感兴趣就继续，不感兴趣就放下
questions.option_b_en: Flip a few pages — if it hooks me, great; if not, drop it
questions.option_c_cn: 从头到尾精读，特别关注每个论点的推理过程
questions.option_c_en: Read it cover to cover, especially tracing every argument's logic

questions.question_cn: 你搬到一个新城市，第一周你会——
questions.question_en: You move to a new city. In the first week you —
questions.option_a_cn: 加入当地社群，和邻居打招呼认识新朋友
questions.option_a_en: Join local groups and meet neighbors to make new friends
questions.option_b_cn: 根据自己的节奏，先把生活必需品安顿好
questions.option_b_en: Get settled at my own pace, prioritize the essentials
questions.option_c_cn: 迅速适应环境，跟谁都能聊两句
questions.option_c_en: Quickly adapt, chat with anyone I meet

questions.question_cn: 你发现一款App有个隐藏的bug——
questions.question_en: You discover a hidden bug in an app —
questions.option_a_cn: 花时间深入研究这个bug的根本原因
questions.option_a_en: Spend time investigating the root cause of the bug
questions.option_b_cn: 立刻反馈给开发者，附上详细的复现步骤
questions.option_b_en: Report it immediately to the developers with detailed steps
questions.option_c_cn: 看看能不能利用这个bug做点有趣的事
questions.option_c_en: See if I can exploit this bug for something fun

questions.question_cn: 餐厅点菜，菜单上有你从没见过的奇怪菜名——
questions.question_en: At a restaurant, the menu has a dish with a bizarre name you've never seen —
questions.option_a_cn: 直接点，不好吃也当一次冒险
questions.option_a_en: Order it — even if it's bad, it's an adventure
questions.option_b_cn: 问服务员这道菜的食材和做法
questions.option_b_en: Ask the waiter about its ingredients and preparation
questions.option_c_cn: 搜搜这道菜的来历和文化背景
questions.option_c_en: Look up the dish's origins and cultural background

questions.question_cn: 你在路上捡到一个未拆封的快递——
questions.question_en: You find an unopened package on the street —
questions.option_a_cn: 看看地址，想办法联系收件人归还
questions.option_a_en: Check the address and try to contact the recipient to return it
questions.option_b_cn: 交给附近的快递站或物业
questions.option_b_en: Drop it off at a nearby delivery station or property office
questions.option_c_cn: 发到社交媒体上帮忙找失主
questions.option_c_en: Post on social media to help find the owner

questions.question_cn: 你需要给一个不太熟的同事写生日祝福——
questions.question_en: You need to write a birthday message for a colleague you don't know well —
questions.option_a_cn: 写一段得体周到的祝福，让对方感到被重视
questions.option_a_en: Write a thoughtful message that makes them feel valued
questions.option_b_cn: 简短真诚就好，不用太复杂
questions.option_b_en: Keep it short and sincere, no need to overcomplicate it
questions.option_c_cn: 写个有创意的搞笑祝福，让大家都乐一乐
questions.option_c_en: Write something creative and funny to give everyone a laugh

questions.question_cn: 你的电脑突然变得很慢——
questions.question_en: Your computer suddenly becomes very slow —
questions.option_a_cn: 打开任务管理器，逐个排查占用资源的进程
questions.option_a_en: Open task manager and check each process consuming resources
questions.option_b_cn: 先重启试试，大部分问题重启就能解决
questions.option_b_en: Restart first — most problems are solved by a reboot
questions.option_c_cn: 查阅相关技术文档，从底层找原因
questions.option_c_en: Read technical documentation to find the underlying cause

questions.question_cn: 你被安排了一个完全陌生的工作任务——
questions.question_en: You're assigned a task in an area you know nothing about —
questions.option_a_cn: 先找这个领域的经典资料系统学习
questions.option_a_en: Start with foundational materials and study the field systematically
questions.option_b_cn: 直接上手做，遇到问题再查
questions.option_b_en: Just start doing it, look things up as problems arise
questions.option_c_cn: 找做过类似任务的同事请教经验
questions.option_c_en: Ask colleagues who've done similar tasks for their experience

questions.question_cn: 朋友邀请你参加一个你完全没接触过的运动——
questions.question_en: A friend invites you to try a sport you've never done —
questions.option_a_cn: 先看看教学视频和规则说明再去
questions.option_a_en: Watch tutorials and learn the rules before going
questions.option_b_cn: 直接去，现场学最快
questions.option_b_en: Just go — learning on the spot is fastest
questions.option_c_cn: 了解一下这项运动的历史和文化再决定
questions.option_c_en: Learn about the sport's history and culture first

questions.question_cn: 你和朋友旅行时迷路了——
questions.question_en: You and your friends get lost during a trip —
questions.option_a_cn: 打开地图，分析几条可能的路线
questions.option_a_en: Open the map and analyze several possible routes
questions.option_b_cn: 随便走走看，迷路本身就是旅行的一部分
questions.option_b_en: Just wander — getting lost is part of the adventure
questions.option_c_cn: 和大家一起商量，投票决定走哪条路
questions.option_c_en: Discuss with everyone and vote on which way to go

questions.question_cn: 你在写一篇重要的报告——
questions.question_en: You're writing an important report —
questions.option_a_cn: 反复检查每个数据和措辞是否准确
questions.option_a_en: Double-check every piece of data and wording for accuracy
questions.option_b_cn: 先写个能交差的版本，有时间再润色
questions.option_b_en: Write a passable version first, polish later if there's time
questions.option_c_cn: 研究这个主题的前沿论文，确保报告有深度
questions.option_c_en: Study cutting-edge papers on the topic to ensure depth

questions.question_cn: 你要装修新房，设计师给了三个方案——
questions.question_en: You're renovating and the designer offers three plans —
questions.option_a_cn: 从实用角度出发，选性价比最高的
questions.option_a_en: Choose the most cost-effective, practical one
questions.option_b_cn: 选最不常见的方案，让家与众不同
questions.option_b_en: Pick the most unconventional one to make the home unique
questions.option_c_cn: 列出每个方案的优缺点，做个对比表
questions.option_c_en: List pros and cons of each plan in a comparison table

questions.question_cn: 你在社交场合遇到一个观点和你完全相反的人——
questions.question_en: At a social event you meet someone with completely opposite views —
questions.option_a_cn: 认真倾听，试着理解对方的逻辑
questions.option_a_en: Listen carefully and try to understand their logic
questions.option_b_cn: 直接表达自己的立场，不会为了和气而让步
questions.option_b_en: State my position directly — I won't back down just to keep peace
questions.option_c_cn: 找到双方能接受的中间地带
questions.option_c_en: Find a middle ground both sides can accept

questions.question_cn: 你发现一个同事工作中犯了个小错误——
questions.question_en: You notice a colleague made a small mistake at work —
questions.option_a_cn: 私下提醒对方，注意维护关系
questions.option_a_en: Quietly let them know in private, maintaining the relationship
questions.option_b_cn: 帮忙检查一下还有没有其他类似的疏忽
questions.option_b_en: Help check if there are other similar oversights
questions.option_c_cn: 立刻指出来，错了就要及时纠正
questions.option_c_en: Point it out immediately — mistakes should be corrected promptly

questions.question_cn: 你有一笔闲钱可以投资——
questions.question_en: You have some spare money to invest —
questions.option_a_cn: 研究过往数据和市场趋势，做出理性判断
questions.option_a_en: Study historical data and market trends to make a rational call
questions.option_b_cn: 选一个大家都认可的稳妥选项
questions.option_b_en: Pick a safe option that everyone agrees on
questions.option_c_cn: 投一个冷门但有潜力的新兴领域
questions.option_c_en: Invest in an obscure but promising emerging field

questions.question_cn: 你加入了一个新的兴趣小组——
questions.question_en: You join a new hobby group —
questions.option_a_cn: 先观察了解规则和氛围再参与
questions.option_a_en: Observe the rules and vibe first before jumping in
questions.option_b_cn: 积极自我介绍，和每个人聊几句
questions.option_b_en: Introduce myself enthusiastically and chat with everyone
questions.option_c_cn: 找到自己能贡献的地方，主动提出帮忙
questions.option_c_en: Find where I can contribute and proactively offer help

questions.question_cn: 你正在追一部很长的连续剧——
questions.question_en: You're binge-watching a very long TV series —
questions.option_a_cn: 跳到最后看结局，再决定要不要补中间的
questions.option_a_en: Skip to the finale, then decide if the middle is worth watching
questions.option_b_cn: 搜索相关幕后花絮和创作理念加深理解
questions.option_b_en: Search for behind-the-scenes info and creative vision to deepen understanding
questions.option_c_cn: 按顺序看，不放过任何一个伏笔和细节
questions.option_c_en: Watch in order, catching every foreshadowing detail

questions.question_cn: 你的好朋友创业，邀请你加入——
questions.question_en: Your close friend starts a business and invites you to join —
questions.option_a_cn: 先评估商业模式和市场前景
questions.option_a_en: First evaluate the business model and market prospects
questions.option_b_cn: 答应，但先说清楚各自的分工和底线
questions.option_b_en: Agree, but first clarify roles and boundaries
questions.option_c_cn: 拉几个靠谱的朋友一起商量
questions.option_c_en: Pull in a few reliable friends to discuss it together

questions.question_cn: 你在网上看到一个很有争议的新闻——
questions.question_en: You see a highly controversial news story online —
questions.option_a_cn: 查阅多个信息源，交叉验证后再下判断
questions.option_a_en: Check multiple sources and cross-verify before forming a view
questions.option_b_cn: 看看评论区大家怎么说
questions.option_b_en: Check the comments to see what everyone thinks
questions.option_c_cn: 直接追根溯源，找到最原始的信息出处
questions.option_c_en: Trace it back to the original source directly

questions.question_cn: 你组织一场聚会，预算有限——
questions.question_en: You're organizing a party on a tight budget —
questions.option_a_cn: 在预算内找到最省钱又不失品质的方案
questions.option_a_en: Find the most cost-effective plan without sacrificing quality
questions.option_b_cn: 搞个打破常规的主题，比花钱更重要的是创意
questions.option_b_en: Create a rule-breaking theme — creativity matters more than money
questions.option_c_cn: 大家AA，每人负责一部分
questions.option_c_en: Split it up — everyone contributes a piece

questions.question_cn: 你在学一门新语言——
questions.question_en: You're learning a new language —
questions.option_a_cn: 研究这门语言的语法体系和发展历史
questions.option_a_en: Study the grammar system and historical evolution of the language
questions.option_b_cn: 直接去和母语者聊天，在实践中学
questions.option_b_en: Start chatting with native speakers — learn by doing
questions.option_c_cn: 下载最高效的App，每天打卡完成任务
questions.option_c_en: Download the most efficient app and complete daily tasks

questions.question_cn: 你的团队需要做一个重大决策——
questions.question_en: Your team needs to make a major decision —
questions.option_a_cn: 确保每个成员都发表了自己的看法
questions.option_a_en: Make sure every team member shares their perspective
questions.option_b_cn: 收集所有相关数据后做综合分析
questions.option_b_en: Gather all relevant data and do a comprehensive analysis
questions.option_c_cn: 按照团队一贯的原则和价值观来判断
questions.option_c_en: Judge based on the team's established principles and values

questions.question_cn: 你收到一份礼物，但你完全用不上——
questions.question_en: You receive a gift you have absolutely no use for —
questions.option_a_cn: 研究一下能不能改造成别的有用的东西
questions.option_a_en: See if I can repurpose it into something useful
questions.option_b_cn: 直说谢谢但坦诚自己用不上，问能不能换
questions.option_b_en: Say thanks honestly but admit I can't use it, ask to exchange
questions.option_c_cn: 转送给真正需要的人
questions.option_c_en: Re-gift it to someone who actually needs it

questions.question_cn: 你要给新员工做一次培训——
questions.question_en: You need to train a new employee —
questions.option_a_cn: 准备一份系统的培训材料，涵盖所有知识点
questions.option_a_en: Prepare systematic training materials covering all key knowledge
questions.option_b_cn: 带着对方边做边学，实践出真知
questions.option_b_en: Learn by doing together — practice beats theory
questions.option_c_cn: 根据对方的背景，调整讲解方式让Ta容易接受
questions.option_c_en: Adjust my teaching style to their background for easier understanding

questions.question_cn: 你在超市看到一个从没见过的零食品牌——
questions.question_en: You spot an unfamiliar snack brand at the supermarket —
questions.option_a_cn: 翻到背面看配料表和营养成分
questions.option_a_en: Flip it over to check the ingredient list and nutrition facts
questions.option_b_cn: 管它呢，买一个尝尝就知道了
questions.option_b_en: Who cares, just buy it and try
questions.option_c_cn: 搜搜这个品牌的背景和口碑评价
questions.option_c_en: Search for the brand's background and reviews

questions.question_cn: 你答应帮朋友搬家，但当天突然下大雨——
questions.question_en: You promised to help a friend move, but it's pouring rain that day —
questions.option_a_cn: 答应了就去，下雨也不是不能搬
questions.option_a_en: I promised, so I'll go — rain doesn't make it impossible
questions.option_b_cn: 发个群消息看看其他朋友谁也能来帮忙
questions.option_b_en: Message the group to see who else can come help
questions.option_c_cn: 快速想个备选方案，比如先搬最怕潮的东西
questions.option_c_en: Quickly think of a backup plan, like moving moisture-sensitive items first

questions.question_cn: 你在图书馆发现一本绝版的古籍——
questions.question_en: You find a rare out-of-print book at the library —
questions.option_a_cn: 仔细翻阅，研究它的版本信息和历史价值
questions.option_a_en: Carefully study its edition info and historical value
questions.option_b_cn: 如果内容有用就快速拍照保存关键章节
questions.option_b_en: If it's useful, quickly photograph the key chapters
questions.option_c_cn: 沉下心来，从头到尾慢慢读，享受探索的过程
questions.option_c_en: Settle in and read from start to finish, enjoying the discovery

questions.question_cn: 周末你有一整天的空闲时间——
questions.question_en: You have a whole free day on the weekend —
questions.option_a_cn: 约上朋友一起做点什么
questions.option_a_en: Call up friends to do something together
questions.option_b_cn: 做一件一直想做但没时间做的事
questions.option_b_en: Finally do that thing I've been meaning to do
questions.option_c_cn: 不做计划，跟着感觉走
questions.option_c_en: No plans — just go with the flow

questions.question_cn: 你在做一道数学难题——
questions.question_en: You're working on a difficult math problem —
questions.option_a_cn: 尝试多种解题思路，从不同角度切入
questions.option_a_en: Try multiple approaches, attacking it from different angles
questions.option_b_cn: 死磕到底，不解出来不罢休
questions.option_b_en: Grind until I solve it — I won't give up
questions.option_c_cn: 先查查有没有类似的经典题型和解法
questions.option_c_en: First check if there are similar classic problems and solutions

questions.question_cn: 你的手机存储空间不够了——
questions.question_en: Your phone storage is full —
questions.option_a_cn: 逐个检查每个App的占用，精确清理
questions.option_a_en: Check each app's storage usage and clean precisely
questions.option_b_cn: 买个更大的手机或云存储，一劳永逸
questions.option_b_en: Buy a bigger phone or cloud storage — solve it once and for all
questions.option_c_cn: 快速删掉明显不需要的大文件
questions.option_c_en: Quickly delete obviously unnecessary large files

questions.question_cn: 你被邀请当婚礼的司仪——
questions.question_en: You're invited to be the MC at a wedding —
questions.option_a_cn: 研究一下婚礼文化和传统仪式流程
questions.option_a_en: Study wedding culture and traditional ceremony procedures
questions.option_b_cn: 根据新人的性格设计个性化流程
questions.option_b_en: Design a personalized program based on the couple's personality
questions.option_c_cn: 先问问新人和亲友有什么想法和期待
questions.option_c_en: Ask the couple and their families about their wishes and expectations

questions.question_cn: 你在咖啡馆工作时，隔壁桌在大声讨论一个你很熟悉的话题——
questions.question_en: While working at a cafe, the next table is loudly discussing a topic you know well —
questions.option_a_cn: 忍不住插一句，分享自己的见解
questions.option_a_en: Can't help but join in and share my perspective
questions.option_b_cn: 戴上耳机，专注自己的事
questions.option_b_en: Put on headphones and focus on my own work
questions.option_c_cn: 默默听一会儿，看看有没有新的观点
questions.option_c_en: Listen quietly for a while, see if there are fresh viewpoints

questions.question_cn: 你需要选一个新的健身方式——
questions.question_en: You need to choose a new workout routine —
questions.option_a_cn: 查阅运动科学研究，选科学证据最强的方式
questions.option_a_en: Check sports science research and pick the most evidence-backed method
questions.option_b_cn: 问问身边健身的朋友都推荐什么
questions.option_b_en: Ask fitness-minded friends what they recommend
questions.option_c_cn: 选个最省时间又有效的方案
questions.option_c_en: Pick the most time-efficient yet effective plan

questions.question_cn: 你看到有人在公共场合插队——
questions.question_en: You see someone cutting in line in public —
questions.option_a_cn: 直接上前说：「请排队」
questions.option_a_en: Walk up and say: 'Please wait in line'
questions.option_b_cn: 用一种幽默的方式提醒对方
questions.option_b_en: Use humor to call them out
questions.option_c_cn: 看看其他人的反应，大家一起说比较有效
questions.option_c_en: See how others react — speaking up together is more effective

questions.question_cn: 你要为公司策划一个年会节目——
questions.question_en: You need to plan a performance for the company annual party —
questions.option_a_cn: 参考往年的经典节目和行业内的成功案例
questions.option_a_en: Reference past classic performances and industry success stories
questions.option_b_cn: 搞个从没人做过的节目，让大家惊喜
questions.option_b_en: Create something nobody's ever done — surprise everyone
questions.option_c_cn: 征集大家的创意，一起策划
questions.option_c_en: Collect everyone's creative ideas and plan together

questions.question_cn: 你的朋友向你借钱——
questions.question_en: A friend asks to borrow money —
questions.option_a_cn: 看情况和金额灵活处理
questions.option_a_en: Handle it flexibly depending on the situation and amount
questions.option_b_cn: 有自己的原则：该借就借，不该借就不借
questions.option_b_en: I have my principles: lend when appropriate, decline when not
questions.option_c_cn: 先了解清楚原因和用途再决定
questions.option_c_en: Understand the reason and purpose clearly before deciding

questions.question_cn: 你在玩密室逃脱——
questions.question_en: You're playing an escape room game —
questions.option_a_cn: 仔细观察房间每个角落，不放过任何线索
questions.option_a_en: Carefully examine every corner of the room, miss nothing
questions.option_b_cn: 和队友分工合作，每人负责一个区域
questions.option_b_en: Split up with teammates, each covering one area
questions.option_c_cn: 尝试最不合常理的解谜思路
questions.option_c_en: Try the most unconventional puzzle-solving approach

questions.question_cn: 你刚到一个新公司，发现流程有很多不合理的地方——
questions.question_en: At your new job, you notice many inefficient processes —
questions.option_a_cn: 先了解为什么流程是这样的，可能有历史原因
questions.option_a_en: First understand why things are this way — there may be historical reasons
questions.option_b_cn: 直接写改进方案提交给领导
questions.option_b_en: Write an improvement proposal and submit it directly
questions.option_c_cn: 从最影响效率的环节开始优化
questions.option_c_en: Start optimizing from the most impactful bottleneck

questions.question_cn: 你养了一盆植物，但它越来越蔫了——
questions.question_en: Your potted plant is slowly wilting —
questions.option_a_cn: 查植物百科，了解这个品种的养护知识
questions.option_a_en: Look up the plant encyclopedia for care knowledge of this species
questions.option_b_cn: 检查土壤、光照、浇水频率，逐一排除问题
questions.option_b_en: Check soil, light, watering frequency — eliminate issues one by one
questions.option_c_cn: 问问花店老板或养花群里的朋友
questions.option_c_en: Ask the florist or friends in a plant-lover group

questions.question_cn: 朋友圈里有人发了一条明显的谣言——
questions.question_en: Someone on your social media shares an obvious rumor —
questions.option_a_cn: 找到权威来源的辟谣信息转发出去
questions.option_a_en: Find authoritative debunking and share it
questions.option_b_cn: 私信对方提醒一下就好
questions.option_b_en: Just send them a private message to let them know
questions.option_c_cn: 深入查证，写一篇详细的辟谣分析
questions.option_c_en: Investigate thoroughly and write a detailed debunking analysis

questions.question_cn: 你在排队买奶茶，排到你的时候发现菜单换了——
questions.question_en: You're in line for bubble tea and discover the menu changed when it's your turn —
questions.option_a_cn: 随便选个看起来顺眼的，别耽误后面的人
questions.option_a_en: Just pick something that looks good, don't hold up the line
questions.option_b_cn: 问店员哪款是招牌，听听推荐
questions.option_b_en: Ask the staff which one is the signature, hear their recommendation
questions.option_c_cn: 让后面的人先点，我研究一下菜单
questions.option_c_en: Let the person behind me go first while I study the menu

questions.question_cn: 你在规划一次长途旅行——
questions.question_en: You're planning a long trip —
questions.option_a_cn: 做详细攻略，每天行程都安排好
questions.option_a_en: Make a detailed itinerary with every day planned out
questions.option_b_cn: 只定大方向，到了再说
questions.option_b_en: Just set the general direction, figure it out when I get there
questions.option_c_cn: 研究目的地的历史文化，规划有深度的路线
questions.option_c_en: Research the destination's history and culture for a meaningful route

questions.question_cn: 你在开会时，有人提出了一个大家都觉得不靠谱的方案——
questions.question_en: In a meeting, someone pitches an idea everyone thinks is unrealistic —
questions.option_a_cn: 认真想想这个方案有没有可取之处
questions.option_a_en: Seriously consider if there's any merit in the idea
questions.option_b_cn: 如果确实不行，直接说出问题所在
questions.option_b_en: If it really doesn't work, point out the issues directly
questions.option_c_cn: 提议大家一起头脑风暴，在这个基础上改进
questions.option_c_en: Suggest everyone brainstorm together to improve on it

questions.question_cn: 你在网上买了个东西，到货发现和图片不太一样——
questions.question_en: You ordered something online and it doesn't match the photos —
questions.option_a_cn: 对比图片和实物的每个差异，写详细的反馈
questions.option_a_en: Compare every difference between the photos and reality, write detailed feedback
questions.option_b_cn: 如果还能用就算了，不想折腾退换
questions.option_b_en: If it still works, just keep it — not worth the hassle of returning
questions.option_c_cn: 直接退货，商家不诚信就不值得支持
questions.option_c_en: Return it immediately — a dishonest seller doesn't deserve my business

questions.question_cn: 你在超市收银台发现自己忘带钱包了——
questions.question_en: At the supermarket checkout you realize you forgot your wallet —
questions.option_a_cn: 问问能不能手机支付或者先欠着下次来还
questions.option_a_en: Ask if I can pay by phone or leave an IOU
questions.option_b_cn: 打电话让朋友送过来
questions.option_b_en: Call a friend to bring it over
questions.option_c_cn: 果断放弃，下次再来买
questions.option_c_en: Just leave and come back next time

questions.question_cn: 你想学做一道新甜品，网上有好多不同版本的教程——
questions.question_en: You want to learn a new dessert recipe — there are tons of different tutorials online —
questions.option_a_cn: 找评分最高的教程直接跟做
questions.option_a_en: Find the highest-rated tutorial and follow it directly
questions.option_b_cn: 看三四个版本，综合出最优配方
questions.option_b_en: Watch several versions and synthesize the best recipe
questions.option_c_cn: 不看教程，凭感觉创造自己的版本
questions.option_c_en: Skip tutorials and create my own version by feel

questions.question_cn: 你的邻居总是在深夜放很吵的音乐——
questions.question_en: Your neighbor keeps playing loud music late at night —
questions.option_a_cn: 直接敲门和对方沟通，说清楚影响
questions.option_a_en: Knock on their door directly and explain the impact
questions.option_b_cn: 了解一下相关的噪音规定，必要时投诉
questions.option_b_en: Look up noise regulations and file a complaint if needed
questions.option_c_cn: 先和其他邻居聊聊，看大家是不是都受影响
questions.option_c_en: Talk to other neighbors first to see if everyone is affected

questions.question_cn: 你发现自己关注了太多社交媒体账号，信息过载了——
questions.question_en: You realize you follow too many social media accounts and feel information overload —
questions.option_a_cn: 逐一审查，取关所有不再有价值的账号
questions.option_a_en: Review each one and unfollow everything that's no longer valuable
questions.option_b_cn: 直接卸载App，来个信息断舍离
questions.option_b_en: Uninstall the app entirely — digital detox
questions.option_c_cn: 分类整理，设置关注列表让信息有序
questions.option_c_en: Categorize and organize into lists for structured information flow

questions.question_cn: 你的同事请你帮忙做一个你不擅长的事——
questions.question_en: A colleague asks for help with something outside your expertise —
questions.option_a_cn: 坦诚说自己不擅长，但帮忙找更合适的人
questions.option_a_en: Honestly say I'm not good at this, but help find the right person
questions.option_b_cn: 试着学一下，说不定能搞定
questions.option_b_en: Try to learn it — maybe I can figure it out
questions.option_c_cn: 先快速了解基础知识，再告诉同事我能帮到什么程度
questions.option_c_en: Quickly learn the basics, then tell my colleague what I can and can't help with

questions.question_cn: 你在地铁上看到有人在读一本你也读过的书——
questions.question_en: On the subway you see someone reading a book you've also read —
questions.option_a_cn: 主动搭话聊聊这本书的感受
questions.option_a_en: Strike up a conversation about the book
questions.option_b_cn: 默默回味自己当初读这本书时的感悟
questions.option_b_en: Quietly recall my own insights from when I read it
questions.option_c_cn: 在心里复述书的主要观点，看自己还记得多少
questions.option_c_en: Mentally review the main arguments to see how much I remember

questions.question_cn: 你参加一个即兴演讲比赛——
questions.question_en: You're in an impromptu speech competition —
questions.option_a_cn: 快速组织逻辑框架，条理清晰地表达
questions.option_a_en: Quickly structure a logical framework and present clearly
questions.option_b_cn: 调动氛围，用幽默和故事打动评委
questions.option_b_en: Set the mood, win judges over with humor and stories
questions.option_c_cn: 引用经典案例或名言来支撑观点
questions.option_c_en: Cite classic cases or famous quotes to support my points

questions.question_cn: 你正在看一场体育比赛，你支持的队落后了——
questions.question_en: You're watching a sports game and your team is losing —
questions.option_a_cn: 分析战术，觉得教练应该换策略
questions.option_a_en: Analyze tactics and think the coach should change strategy
questions.option_b_cn: 继续大声加油，比赛没结束就有希望
questions.option_b_en: Keep cheering loudly — it's not over until it's over
questions.option_c_cn: 和旁边的球迷讨论比赛走势
questions.option_c_en: Discuss the game's trajectory with fans around me

questions.question_cn: 你在整理房间时发现了一堆旧照片——
questions.question_en: While cleaning your room you find a pile of old photos —
questions.option_a_cn: 按时间线整理，分类归档保存好
questions.option_a_en: Organize them chronologically and archive them properly
questions.option_b_cn: 发到家族群里，和大家一起回忆往事
questions.option_b_en: Share them in the family group chat to reminisce together
questions.option_c_cn: 细细翻看每一张，回忆照片背后的故事
questions.option_c_en: Look through each one carefully, remembering the stories behind them

questions.question_cn: 你在考虑要不要换工作——
questions.question_en: You're thinking about changing jobs —
questions.option_a_cn: 列出当前工作和新机会的所有优劣势对比
questions.option_a_en: List all pros and cons of current job vs the new opportunity
questions.option_b_cn: 听听过来人的经验和建议
questions.option_b_en: Listen to experienced people's advice and stories
questions.option_c_cn: 如果直觉告诉我该走，那就行动
questions.option_c_en: If my gut says go, then go for it

questions.question_cn: 你在家自己修理一个坏了的东西——
questions.question_en: You're trying to fix something broken at home —
questions.option_a_cn: 先搞清楚坏的原理，找到根本原因
questions.option_a_en: First understand the mechanism and find the root cause
questions.option_b_cn: 找到最快的方法修好就行
questions.option_b_en: Find the quickest fix and call it done
questions.option_c_cn: 趁机改造一下，让它比原来更好用
questions.option_c_en: Take the chance to modify it and make it better than before

questions.question_cn: 你在一个陌生城市问路——
questions.question_en: You're asking for directions in an unfamiliar city —
questions.option_a_cn: 用手机导航，自己找到目的地
questions.option_a_en: Use GPS and find the destination myself
questions.option_b_cn: 找路人问路，顺便聊几句当地见闻
questions.option_b_en: Ask a local for directions and chat about the area
questions.option_c_cn: 故意不看导航，享受在陌生地方随便走走的感觉
questions.option_c_en: Skip the GPS on purpose — enjoy the feeling of wandering

questions.question_cn: 你被要求在一周内学会一个全新的软件——
questions.question_en: You need to learn a completely new software tool within a week —
questions.option_a_cn: 按官方教程从基础到进阶系统学习
questions.option_a_en: Follow the official tutorial systematically from basic to advanced
questions.option_b_cn: 直接打开软件摸索，遇到不会的再搜
questions.option_b_en: Open the software and explore, search when I get stuck
questions.option_c_cn: 找会用的人远程指导我
questions.option_c_en: Find someone who knows it and get remote guidance

questions.question_cn: 你写了一篇文章发到网上，有人留了一条很尖锐的批评——
questions.question_en: You post an article online and someone leaves a harsh critique —
questions.option_a_cn: 认真思考批评是否有道理，有则改之
questions.option_a_en: Seriously consider if the criticism has merit and improve if so
questions.option_b_cn: 直接回复，坦诚交流不同观点
questions.option_b_en: Reply directly, exchanging different views candidly

advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 标准祝福就好，不用过度
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Standard wishes are fine — don't overdo it
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 找个有趣的表情包或段子
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Find a fun meme or joke to send
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 问一下关系好的同事他/她喜欢什么
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Ask a closer colleague what they like

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在考虑要不要删除某个社交媒体账号——
advanced_questions.question_en: You're considering deleting a social media account —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 删！精简生活从减少干扰开始
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Delete it! Simplify life by reducing distractions
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 先停用一段时间看看感受
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Deactivate for a while and see how you feel
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 不删但减少使用时间
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Keep it but reduce usage time

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你坐长途飞机旁边是个话很多的人——
advanced_questions.question_en: You're on a long flight and your seatmate is very chatty —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 礼貌聊几句然后戴上耳机
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Chat politely for a bit then put on headphones
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 也许能聊出有趣的故事
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Maybe interesting stories will come up
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 直接戴耳机表示不想聊天
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Put on headphones immediately to signal no-chat

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你连续看了三个小时手机后感到空虚——
advanced_questions.question_en: You feel empty after three hours of mindless phone scrolling —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 放下手机出门走走
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Put the phone down and go for a walk
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 想想为什么会无意识地刷这么久
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Reflect on why you scrolled unconsciously for so long
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 切换到做点有意义的事
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Switch to doing something meaningful

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你正在专注做事时被人打断——
advanced_questions.question_en: You're in deep focus when someone interrupts you —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 快速回应然后立刻回到工作
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Respond quickly then immediately get back to work
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 有点烦但还是耐心处理
advanced_questions.option_b_en: A bit annoyed but handle it patiently
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 先把手头的记下来再去应对
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Jot down where you left off before responding

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你参加了一个培训课但觉得内容太基础——
advanced_questions.question_en: You attend a training session but the content is too basic —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 还是认真听可能有新角度
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Listen carefully — there might be new angles
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 利用这个时间做自己的事
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Use the time to work on your own stuff
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 跟讲师提问一些进阶问题
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Ask the instructor some advanced questions

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在做一件事时突然有了一个更好的想法——
advanced_questions.question_en: While working on something, you suddenly get a much better idea —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 先记下来，完成手头的再说
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Note it down and finish what you're doing first
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 立刻切换到新想法
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Switch to the new idea immediately
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 快速评估新想法是否真的更好
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Quickly evaluate whether the new idea is actually better

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你要搬家但东西太多了——
advanced_questions.question_en: You need to move but have too much stuff —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 趁机断舍离扔掉一半
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Use this chance to declutter and throw out half
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 全部打包慢慢收拾
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Pack everything and sort later
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 问朋友需不需要顺便送人
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Ask friends if they want any of your stuff

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你的计划被突发事件完全打乱——
advanced_questions.question_en: An unexpected event completely disrupts your plan —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 马上做一个新计划
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Immediately make a new plan
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 先处理眼前最紧急的事
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Handle the most urgent thing first
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 深呼吸，船到桥头自然直
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Take a deep breath — things will work themselves out

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在一个无聊的等待中（比如排队、等人）——
advanced_questions.question_en: You're stuck in a boring wait (like queuing or waiting for someone) —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 刷手机打发时间
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Scroll your phone to pass the time
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 利用时间思考或计划
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Use the time to think or plan
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 观察周围发生的事
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Observe what's happening around you

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你想开始一个副业——
advanced_questions.question_en: You want to start a side hustle —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 从自己最擅长的领域开始
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Start from your area of expertise
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 选市场需求最大的方向
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Choose the direction with highest market demand
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 先小规模试错再决定
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Start small, test, and iterate

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在做一个团队项目但有人不出力——
advanced_questions.question_en: You're in a team project but someone isn't pulling their weight —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 直接找ta谈分工
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Talk to them directly about the workload split
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 把ta的活也做了，项目更重要
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Pick up their slack — the project matters more
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 反映给组长/老师处理
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Report it to the team leader/instructor

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你意外收到一封很久以前发出的申请的回复——
advanced_questions.question_en: You unexpectedly hear back about an application you sent ages ago —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 虽然已经不需要了但还是去了解一下
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Even though you don't need it anymore, check it out
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 已经过了那个阶段了直接拒绝
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Already past that stage — decline directly
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 评估现在这个机会是否仍然有价值
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Evaluate whether the opportunity still has value

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你发现一个你一直相信的「常识」其实是错的——
advanced_questions.question_en: You discover a 'common sense' belief you've always held is actually wrong —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 立刻更新自己的认知
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Immediately update your understanding
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 再查几个来源确认一下
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Check a few more sources to confirm
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 想想自己还有哪些可能是错的
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Wonder what else you might be wrong about

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你的老朋友最近变了很多你有点不适应——
advanced_questions.question_en: Your old friend has changed a lot recently and you feel uncomfortable —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 人都会变，接受新的他/她
advanced_questions.option_a_en: People change — accept the new them
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 坦诚地聊聊你的感受
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Honestly share how you feel
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 慢慢调整期望，减少接触也没关系
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Adjust expectations gradually — less contact is okay

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在工作中发现了一个可以走的捷径但不太正规——
advanced_questions.question_en: You discover a shortcut at work that's effective but not quite by-the-book —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 按规矩来，捷径有风险
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Follow the rules — shortcuts carry risk
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 用了，但记录下来以后规范化
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Use it but document it for future formalization
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 问领导这样做行不行
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Ask your manager whether this approach is acceptable

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你发现自己的性格跟父母越来越像——
advanced_questions.question_en: You notice you're becoming more and more like your parents —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 挺好的，他们身上有很多优点
advanced_questions.option_a_en: That's great — they have many good qualities
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 有点慌，刻意做出改变
advanced_questions.option_b_en: A bit alarmed — consciously make changes
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 接受那些好的部分改掉不好的
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Accept the good parts, work on the less good

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你参加了一个线上投票觉得结果不公——
advanced_questions.question_en: You participated in an online vote and feel the result was unfair —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 接受结果少数服从多数
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Accept it — majority rules
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 在评论区理性表达不同意见
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Rationally express your dissent in the comments
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 算了，网络投票本来就不靠谱
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Oh well — online votes are unreliable anyway

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在深夜突然有了一个绝妙的想法——
advanced_questions.question_en: You have a brilliant idea in the middle of the night —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 赶紧记下来明天再说
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Jot it down quickly and revisit tomorrow
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 兴奋得睡不着直接开始做
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Too excited to sleep — start working on it now
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 先验证一下是不是真的好
advanced_questions.option_c_en: First verify whether it's actually a good idea

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你要在两个城市之间选择定居——
advanced_questions.question_en: You need to choose between two cities to settle in —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 各去住一段时间体验
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Live in each for a while to experience them
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 列出所有因素打分对比
advanced_questions.option_b_en: List all factors and score them comparatively
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 选离家人近的
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Choose the one closer to family

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在健身房看到一个器材不会用——
advanced_questions.question_en: You see gym equipment you don't know how to use —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 看看旁边有没有使用说明
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Check if there are instructions nearby
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 问工作人员或旁边的人
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Ask the staff or someone nearby
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 不会用就用别的，没必要纠结
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Use something else — no need to stress

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你想对一个人表白但怕被拒绝——
advanced_questions.question_en: You want to confess your feelings but fear rejection —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 直接说，不说永远没机会
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Just say it — you'll never have a chance if you don't
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 先试探一下对方的态度
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Test the waters first
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 做好被拒绝的心理准备再开口
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Mentally prepare for rejection, then speak up

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在打游戏时队友犯了一个低级错误——
advanced_questions.question_en: Your teammate makes an obvious mistake in a game —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 鼓励一下继续打
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Encourage them and keep playing
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 忍住不说什么
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Hold your tongue and say nothing
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 善意提醒可以怎么改进
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Kindly suggest how to improve

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在考虑要不要学一门乐器——
advanced_questions.question_en: You're thinking about learning a musical instrument —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 先租一个试试看喜不喜欢
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Rent one first to see if you like it
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 找老师系统学习
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Find a teacher for structured learning
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: YouTube自学先入门
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Self-teach on YouTube to start

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你发现你最喜欢的一家店要关了——
advanced_questions.question_en: You discover your favorite shop is closing —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 最后去一次好好告别
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Visit one last time for a proper goodbye
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 问问能不能买到他们的独家产品
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Ask if you can buy their exclusive products
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 在网上给他们写个好评纪念一下
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Write a nice online review as a tribute

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你要帮朋友搬家——
advanced_questions.question_en: You're helping a friend move —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 提前到帮忙规划
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Arrive early and help plan
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 让朋友安排你做什么就做什么
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Do whatever your friend asks you to do
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 自带工具和零食做好准备
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Bring tools and snacks — come prepared

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在考虑要不要告诉朋友ta身上有个尴尬的问题——
advanced_questions.question_en: You're considering whether to tell a friend about an embarrassing issue —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 说，真朋友就该说真话
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Tell them — real friends tell the truth
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 找个委婉的方式提醒
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Find a tactful way to bring it up
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 不说，不是什么大事
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Don't mention it — it's not a big deal

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在公司卫生间听到有人在哭——
advanced_questions.question_en: You hear someone crying in the office restroom —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 敲门问一下需不需要帮助
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Knock and ask if they need help
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 安静离开给对方空间
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Quietly leave and give them space
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 在门口等一下看ta出来要不要聊
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Wait outside and offer to chat when they come out

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你做饭时不小心放多了盐——
advanced_questions.question_en: You accidentally added too much salt while cooking —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 加水或加糖补救
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Add water or sugar to fix it
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 将就吃吧下次注意
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Eat it as-is and be careful next time
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 重新做一遍
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Start over from scratch

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在电梯里只有你和一个你认识但不太熟的人——
advanced_questions.question_en: You're in an elevator with just one acquaintance you barely know —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 微笑打招呼然后安静等着
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Smile, say hi, and wait quietly
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 找个话题闲聊一下
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Find something to chat about
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 低头看手机避免尴尬
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Look at your phone to avoid awkwardness

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你连续刷到三条负面新闻心情变差了——
advanced_questions.question_en: Three negative news stories in a row tank your mood —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 关掉新闻APP做点别的
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Close the news app and do something else
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 提醒自己世界不只是这样的
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Remind yourself the world isn't only like this
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 看一下有没有相关的正面进展
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Check if there are positive developments related to these

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你在购物时售货员一直跟着你推荐——
advanced_questions.question_en: A salesperson keeps following you around and recommending things —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 礼貌说不需要请给我空间
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Politely say you don't need help and ask for space
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 听听推荐也许有用
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Listen to recommendations — they might be useful
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 直接走去另一家店
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Just leave and go to another store

advanced_questions.question_cn: 老板临时让你负责一个完全陌生的项目——
advanced_questions.question_en: Your boss suddenly puts you in charge of a completely unfamiliar project —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 先答应下来，边做边学
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Accept first, learn as you go
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 花一天做个详细计划再开始
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Spend a day making a detailed plan before starting
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 先找相关的人了解情况
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Find relevant people and get context first

advanced_questions.question_cn: 同事在群里发了一个方案，你觉得有明显漏洞——
advanced_questions.question_en: A colleague shares a proposal in the group chat with an obvious flaw —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 私聊指出问题，给个改进建议
advanced_questions.option_a_en: DM them with the issue and a suggestion
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 先自己验证一下是不是真的有问题
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Verify on your own first whether it's actually flawed
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 在群里温和地提出不同看法
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Gently raise a different perspective in the group

advanced_questions.question_cn: 面试官问你「你最大的缺点是什么」——
advanced_questions.question_en: An interviewer asks 'What's your biggest weakness?' —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 真诚地说一个正在改进的缺点
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Honestly share a weakness you're working on
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 用一个已经克服的案例来回答
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Answer with a weakness you've already overcome
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 反问面试官觉得这个岗位需要什么
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Ask the interviewer what the role really needs

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你负责的项目提前完成了，还有一周空闲——
advanced_questions.question_en: Your project finishes a week early, leaving free time —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 帮别的同事分担工作
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Help other teammates with their workload
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 研究一个一直想深入的技术
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Deep-dive into a technology you've been curious about
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 优化项目里可以做得更好的地方
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Refine the parts that could be better

advanced_questions.question_cn: 开会时两个同事吵起来了——
advanced_questions.question_en: Two colleagues start arguing in a meeting —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 打断一下，把话题拉回来
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Interrupt and redirect back to the agenda
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 默默记下双方的观点，会后找人聊
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Note both sides, follow up privately later
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 开个玩笑缓和气氛
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Crack a joke to ease the tension

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你升职后发现下属比你年纪大、经验多——
advanced_questions.question_en: After a promotion, you discover your reports are older and more experienced —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 坦诚交流，表示互相学习
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Have an honest talk about learning from each other
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 用实际成果证明自己
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Prove yourself through concrete results
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 先观察一段时间，了解每个人的风格
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Observe for a while and understand each person's style

advanced_questions.question_cn: 公司要裁员，你的团队必须少一个人——
advanced_questions.question_en: The company is downsizing — your team must lose one person —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 看绩效数据，用事实说话
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Look at performance data and let facts decide
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 跟每个人单独谈，了解他们的情况
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Talk to each person individually about their situation
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 和HR一起制定公平的评估标准
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Work with HR to create fair evaluation criteria

advanced_questions.question_cn: 你被分配到一个你觉得毫无意义的任务——
advanced_questions.question_en: You're assigned a task you think is completely pointless —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 先做了再说，也许做着做着就有意义了
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Just do it — maybe meaning will emerge along the way
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 直接跟领导说出你的疑虑
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Voice your concerns directly to the boss
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 试着找到这个任务隐藏的价值
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Try to find hidden value in the task

advanced_questions.question_cn: 年终总结要写自我评价——
advanced_questions.question_en: It's time to write your year-end self-evaluation —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 把所有做过的事列出来，让数据说话
advanced_questions.option_a_en: List everything you did and let the numbers speak
advanced_questions.option_b_cn: 重点写几个有深度的案例分析
advanced_questions.option_b_en: Focus on a few in-depth case studies
advanced_questions.option_c_cn: 问问同事对自己的评价参考一下
advanced_questions.option_c_en: Ask colleagues for their perspective as reference

advanced_questions.question_cn: 客户突然提出一个不合理的需求——
advanced_questions.question_en: A client suddenly makes an unreasonable request —
advanced_questions.option_a_cn: 先说好的，然后引导他到合理方案
advanced_questions.option_a_en: Say yes first, then guide them to a reasonable plan
